stimulus: Physicist Vera Rubin performed ______blank work on galaxy rotation in the 1970s and 1980s, conducting key observations that later researchers used as the basis for the modern understanding of dark matter.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale: Choice D is correct because "pioneering" means foundational or trailblazing, matching the idea that Rubin’s work served as the basis for later studies. Choice A is incorrect because supplementary suggests her work was secondary, not central. Choice B is incorrect because the work was original. Choice C is incorrect because ineffective contradicts the description.
